questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: It is known that $\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}= \begin{cases}\frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\cos x}}+\frac{\sqrt{\cos x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}} & \text { if } 0 \lt x \lt \frac{\pi}{2} \\ \frac{\sqrt{-\sin x}}{\sqrt{-\cos x}}+\frac{\sqrt{-\cos x}}{\sqrt{-\sin x}} & \text { if } \pi \lt x \lt \frac{3 \pi}{2}\end{cases}$Question: The value of integral $1=\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}) \mathrm{dx}$, where $x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \cup\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)$
questions.solution:
For interval $\mathrm{x} \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & 1=\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}) d x=\int\left(\frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\cos x}}+\frac{\sqrt{\cos x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}}\right) d x \\ & =\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{\sin x \cos x}} d x=\int \sqrt{2} \frac{(\sin x+\cos x)}{\sqrt{\sin 2 x}} d x \\ & =\sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{1-(\sin x-\cos x)^2}} d x=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x-\cos x)+c\end{aligned}$
Put $\sin x-\cos x=1$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x-\cos x)+\mathrm{c} \quad \mathrm{x} \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\ & \mathrm{I}=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x-\cos x}{\sqrt{1-(\sin x-\cos x)^2}}\right) \\ & \mathrm{I}=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x-\cos x)}{\sqrt{2} \sqrt{\sin x \cos x})}\right)+9 \\ & \mathrm{I}=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{\tan x}(-\sqrt{\cos x} x}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+\mathrm{c}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \text { for interval } x \in\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right) \\ & I=\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}) d x=-\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{\sin x \cos x}} d x \\ & I=-\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x-\cos x)+c \\ & I=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\cos x-\sin x)+c \quad x \in\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right) \\ & I=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\cos x-\sin x}{\sqrt{1-(\cos x-\sin x)^2}}\right)+c \\ & I=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\cos x-\sin x}{\sqrt{2} \sqrt{\sin x \cos x}}\right)+c \\ & I=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{\tan x}-\sqrt{\cot x}}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+c \quad x \in\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\because \sqrt{\tan x}=\frac{\sqrt{-\sin x}}{\sqrt{-\cos x}}, \sqrt{\cot x}=\frac{\sqrt{-\cos x}}{\sqrt{-\sin x}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph If $f(x)=\frac{x}{x^2+x+1}$, then On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question If $\int f\left(x^2\right) \mathrm{d} x+\int f\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^2}\right) \mathrm{d}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)=\lambda \ln \left|\frac{\mathrm{x}^2-\mathrm{x}+1}{\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{x}+1}\right|+\mathrm{c}$, then $\lambda$ is -
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \int \frac{x^2 d x}{\left(x^4+x^2+1\right)}+\int x^2\left(\frac{1}{x^4}+\frac{1}{x^2}+1\right)-\left(\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x \\ & =\int \frac{\left(x^2-1\right) d x}{\left(x^4+x^2+1\right)}=\int \frac{\left(1-\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x}{\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}+1\right)} \\ & =\int \frac{\left(1-\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x}{\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)^2-1} \quad \text { Let } x+\frac{1}{x}=t \\ & =\int \frac{d t}{\left(t^2-1\right)}=\frac{1}{2} \ln \left(\frac{t-1}{t+1}\right)=\frac{1}{2} \ln \left(\frac{x+\frac{1}{x}-1}{x+\frac{1}{x}+1}\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \ln \left(\frac{x^2-x+1}{x^2+x+1}\right)+c\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: It is known that $\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}= \begin{cases}\frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\cos x}}+\frac{\sqrt{\cos x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}} & \text { if } 0 \lt x \lt \frac{\pi}{2} \\ \frac{\sqrt{-\sin x}}{\sqrt{-\cos x}}+\frac{\sqrt{-\cos x}}{\sqrt{-\sin x}} & \text { if } \pi \lt x \lt \frac{3 \pi}{2}\end{cases}$Question: The value of integral $I=\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}) d x$, where $x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$
questions.solution:
For interval $\mathrm{x} \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & 1=\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}) d x=\int\left(\frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\cos x}}+\frac{\sqrt{\cos x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}}\right) d x \\ & =\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{\sin x \cos x}} d x=\int \sqrt{2} \frac{(\sin x+\cos x)}{\sqrt{\sin 2 x}} d x \\ & =\sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{1-(\sin x-\cos x)^2}} d x=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x-\cos x)+c\end{aligned}$
Put $\sin x-\cos x=1$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x-\cos x)+\mathrm{c} \quad \mathrm{x} \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\ & \mathrm{I}=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x-\cos x}{\sqrt{1-(\sin x-\cos x)^2}}\right) \\ & \mathrm{I}=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x-\cos x)}{\sqrt{2} \sqrt{\sin x \cos x})}\right)+9 \\ & \mathrm{I}=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{\tan x}(-\sqrt{\cos x} x}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+\mathrm{c}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \text { for interval } x \in\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right) \\ & I=\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}) d x=-\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{\sin x \cos x}} d x \\ & I=-\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x-\cos x)+c \\ & I=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\cos x-\sin x)+c \quad x \in\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right) \\ & I=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\cos x-\sin x}{\sqrt{1-(\cos x-\sin x)^2}}\right)+c \\ & I=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\cos x-\sin x}{\sqrt{2} \sqrt{\sin x \cos x}}\right)+c \\ & I=\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{\tan x}-\sqrt{\cot x}}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+c \quad x \in\left(\pi, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\because \sqrt{\tan x}=\frac{\sqrt{-\sin x}}{\sqrt{-\cos x}}, \sqrt{\cot x}=\frac{\sqrt{-\cos x}}{\sqrt{-\sin x}}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph If $f(x)=\frac{x}{x^2+x+1}$, then On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question If $\int f(\tan x) d x=x-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}(g(x))+c$, then for $x \in\left(-\frac{\pi}{3}, \frac{\pi}{3}\right)$ range of $g(x)$ is -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \int \frac{\tan x d x}{\left(\tan ^2 x+\tan x+1\right)} \\
& =\int \frac{\tan x \sec ^2 x d x}{\left(1+\tan ^2 x\right)\left(1+\tan ^2 x+\tan x\right)}
\end{aligned}$

Let $\tan \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{t}$
$\sec ^2 x d x=d t$
$=\int \frac{t d t}{\left(1+t^2\right)\left(1+t^2+t\right)}=\int \frac{\left(1+t^2+t\right)-\left(1+t^2\right)}{\left(1+t^2\right)\left(1+t^2+t\right)} d t$
$\begin{aligned} & =\int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\left(\mathrm{t}^2+1\right)}-\int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\left(1+\mathrm{t}^2+\mathrm{t}\right)} \\ & =\tan ^{-1}(\mathrm{t})-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \mathrm{t}+1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \\ & =\mathrm{x}-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \tan \mathrm{x}+1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)+\mathrm{c} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{2 \tan \mathrm{x}+1}{\sqrt{3}} \\ & =-\sqrt{3} \lt \tan \mathrm{x} \lt \sqrt{3} \\ & -\frac{2 \sqrt{3}+1}{\sqrt{3}} \lt \frac{2 \tan \mathrm{x}+1}{\sqrt{3}} \lt \frac{2 \sqrt{3}+1}{\sqrt{3}} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}-2 \lt \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x}) \lt \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}+2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
